name: Mintvoid
origin: Chile, in ultra-dry, fluoride-laced salt caves beneath the Atacama Desert's dark-sky observatories
lore:
Mintvoid is a thixotropic, mint-cooled compound that spawns a bounded micro-singularity, drawing in plaque, odor molecules, and stray particulates while capping tidal forces at enamel-safe levels.

Developed when a fluoride crystal matrix was used to quench Hawking noise in a tabletop analogue black hole, it quickly gained fame among observatory crews for cleaning both teeth and delicate optics without residue.

Folklore among night-shift astronomers claims it shortens the 'half-life' of coffee breath and late-hour fatigue.
